acceptance_targets: [ch06-booker-001] PASS if the revised passage in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:289-333 contains (1) one explicit debt datum spoken aloud and (2) one same-scene material consequence that changes food/fuel access before the bed transition; FAIL if the scene still relies on future-tense control promises only.

[ch06-craft-001] PASS if those revised spans include at least one concrete coercive act and one immediate bodily/social consequence for Elsie before the time jump, with no reassurance clause equivalent to 'not within striking distance' or 'not dramatic'; FAIL otherwise.

[ch06-craft-002] PASS if the revised end-scene includes one explicit, actionable risk to Noreen's supply line and one explicit counter-action by Elsie before the final paragraph; FAIL if conflict remains mostly rhetorical.

[ch06-dialogue-001] PASS if, in the revised 241-311 span, at least four key turns show character-distinct rhythm or diction under interruption and no more than one standalone proverb-style line remains; FAIL if both voices still trade similarly polished slogans.

[ch06-prose-001] PASS if the revised cited spans contain no more than three total 'as if' constructions and each surviving comparison does distinct narrative work; FAIL if repeated comparison templates remain the default sentence engine.

[ch06-prose-002] PASS if the revised 221-259 span keeps the control/inventory conflict legible while introducing at least two non-ledger pressure details (bodily, social, or environmental) and avoiding repetitive 'numbers/count/account' phrasing on consecutive turns; FAIL if the exchange still reads as repetitive bookkeeping diction.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:289-333 contains (1) one explicit debt datum spoken aloud and (2) one same-scene material consequence that changes food/fuel access before the bed transition; FAIL if the scene still relies on future-tense control promises only.
findings.problem: The climactic argument withholds concrete debt information and defers the practical consequence to 'tomorrow,' so the chapter closes on posture rather than a fully dramatized causal turn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the confrontation span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:289, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:301, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:323, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:333 so one irreversible consequence lands on-page: either Tom states a specific debt fact (creditor/amount/deadline) or he enacts an immediate restriction that changes tonight's household logistics before bed. Keep the ending image, but make it aftermath, not substitute action.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if those revised spans include at least one concrete coercive act and one immediate bodily/social consequence for Elsie before the time jump, with no reassurance clause equivalent to 'not within striking distance' or 'not dramatic'; FAIL otherwise.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift weakens the coercive-control beat: the narration inserts safety-valve qualifiers ('did not come within striking distance,' 'not dramatic') and then cuts to 'Later, in bed' instead of fully rendering immediate embodied fallout.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:231, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:233, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:265, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:267, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:305, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:327 to replace explanatory cushioning with concrete physical and social action. Show coercive pressure through blocking, proximity, objects, and response, then carry that consequence directly into the bed scene.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised end-scene includes one explicit, actionable risk to Noreen's supply line and one explicit counter-action by Elsie before the final paragraph; FAIL if conflict remains mostly rhetorical.
findings.problem: Timidity after Elsie's confession shifts into aphoristic sparring, which blunts immediate risk calculus around Noreen and the hidden supplies.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the end-scene anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:311, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:333 so at least one exchange names concrete retaliatory risk (for example, a check next door, credit retaliation, or ration lockout) and Elsie makes a tactical counter-move tied to protecting Noreen.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised 241-311 span, at least four key turns show character-distinct rhythm or diction under interruption and no more than one standalone proverb-style line remains; FAIL if both voices still trade similarly polished slogans.
findings.problem: Under peak pressure, Elsie and Tom intermittently converge into polished thesis lines and proverb-like retorts, reducing register separation and raw spoken texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the argument anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:241, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:243, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:307, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:311 to keep high-contraction speech but add interruptions, overlap, and idiolect-specific diction (Tom's work/betting evasions; Elsie's practical scarcity language) in place of mutual aphorisms.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited spans contain no more than three total 'as if' constructions and each surviving comparison does distinct narrative work; FAIL if repeated comparison templates remain the default sentence engine.
findings.problem: Recurring comparison scaffolds ('as if' and repeated simile frames) appear often enough to read as an autopilot sentence habit rather than selective emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Across the cited lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:153, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:181, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:265, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:301, keep only the strongest comparisons and replace the rest with direct sensory or action-based description tied to immediate scene pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised 221-259 span keeps the control/inventory conflict legible while introducing at least two non-ledger pressure details (bodily, social, or environmental) and avoiding repetitive 'numbers/count/account' phrasing on consecutive turns; FAIL if the exchange still reads as repetitive bookkeeping diction.
findings.problem: Lexical over-reuse of counting/accounting language tracks the style-bible stress tell too literally, flattening tonal range in the back half of the chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the control exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:233, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:247, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:249, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:259 so the accounting motif remains present but is no longer the dominant lexical track; convert several ledger terms into concrete bodily, social, or environmental pressure details.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
